name: Anunciada Trail (PR4)
region: Madalena e Beselga e Beselga e Beselga
description: Hike the moderate 6.5-mile Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) circular route in Madeira, exploring levadas, forests, and viewpoints.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Madalena e Beselga e Beselga e Beselga are situated within the municipality of Tomar, Portugal, an area characterized by a diverse natural landscape. The region features hills, valleys, and proximity to the River Zêzere, which forms a large artificial lake due to the Castelo do Bode dam. This environment provides varied terrain for outdoor activities, including areas with forests and river beaches. The natural setting offers opportunities for exploring on foot, with routes often connecting natural features with historical sites. Best easy hiking trails around Madalena e Beselga e Beselga e Beselga The most popular easy hiking route is Mata Nacional dos Sete Montes - circular route in Tomar, a 2.1 miles (3.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This circular route explores the Mata Nacional dos Sete Montes park within Tomar. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mouchão Park – Mouchão Hydraulic Wheel loop from Tomar, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail follows the banks of the Nabão River, passing by the Mouchão Hydraulic Wheel. Local hikers also love the Nuns' Arch of Tomar – Mouchão Park loop from Tomar, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through urban green spaces and along the river, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Hiking around Madalena e Beselga e Beselga e Beselga is defined by riverine paths, forested areas, and routes connecting to historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy trails. The routes in Madalena e Beselga e Beselga e Beselga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 580 hikers have used komoot to explore Madalena e Beselga e Beselga e Beselga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Madalena e Beselga?
A: There are 13 easy hiking trails around Madalena e Beselga. In total, the region offers 26 routes, catering to various difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in this region?
A: The easy hikes around Madalena e Beselga feature varied terrain, including riverine paths along the Nabão River, forested areas, and routes that connect natural features with historical sites. You'll find paths through hills and valleys, often with gentle elevation changes suitable for a relaxed walk.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hikes?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2249715895/mata-nacional-dos-sete-montes-circular-route-in-tomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata Nacional dos Sete Montes - circular route in Tomar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 2.1-mile loop through a park, offering a pleasant and accessible experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these easy hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, many outdoor areas in Portugal are dog-friendly, especially on leash. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them. Trails through natural parks or protected areas might have specific rules, so check local signage.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks available?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32134328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Quinta de Santo André&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant 3-mile loop. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2249715895/mata-nacional-dos-sete-montes-circular-route-in-tomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata Nacional dos Sete Montes - circular route in Tomar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along easy hiking trails?
A: Many easy trails connect with significant historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32143849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuns' Arch of Tomar – Mouchão Park loop from Tomar&lt;/a&gt; takes you through urban green spaces and along the river, near the historic center. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata Nacional dos Sete Montes&lt;/a&gt; park, which features in several easy routes, is adjacent to the UNESCO World Heritage site, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2967545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Christ and Castle of Tomar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural features like viewpoints or river beaches on these easy hikes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can find riverine paths along the Zêzere and Nabão rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7703900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobreirinho River Beach&lt;/a&gt; is a lovely spot to visit. For viewpoints, consider routes that offer glimpses of the surrounding hilly landscapes, such as those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6373631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimo da Lagoa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Madalena e Beselga?
A: The region is pleasant for hiking during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during that season.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Madalena e Beselga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil riverine paths, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the accessibility of the easy trails.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer views of the Castelo do Bode Dam or River Zêzere?
A: While Madalena e Beselga is close to the River Zêzere and Castelo do Bode Dam, specific easy trails directly within the guide might not offer direct dam views. However, the broader region of Tomar provides numerous trails along the Zêzere, offering stunning river landscapes and opportunities to explore the area around the dam.

Q: Where can I find parking for these easy hiking trails?
A: Many trailheads, especially those starting near urban centers like Tomar or popular natural parks, typically have designated parking areas. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouchão Park – Mouchão Hydraulic Wheel loop from Tomar&lt;/a&gt;, parking is usually available near the park entrances or in nearby town parking facilities.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the easy hiking trails?
A: Given the proximity to Tomar, you'll find various cafes and restaurants within a short distance of many trailheads. Routes that pass through or start near Tomar's city center, such as those exploring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata Nacional dos Sete Montes&lt;/a&gt; or Mouchão Park, offer easy access to dining options before or after your hike.
tips: 6 km long aqueduct that is still accessible. Sometimes after the rain, water also collects in the channel, in which water used to flow.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) — circular route?
faqs.answer: The Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) is approximately 10.5 km (6.5 miles) long. Komoot estimates it takes around 2 hours and 51 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as moderate. While some sections follow relatively flat levada paths, the overall distance and elevation gain (around 149 meters up) contribute to its moderate classification. It's accessible to a wide range of hikers, including those seeking a less strenuous experience than some of Madeira's more challenging trails.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4)?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows a 'levada' (irrigation channel) through beautiful forested areas. You'll encounter high-altitude vegetation like tree heath and mountain grapevines. The path is generally well-marked, with some sections being relatively flat, offering a tranquil walking experience through lush landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll find a privileged panoramic view over the valley of the Ribeira de Santa Luzia and the city of Funchal. There's also the Barreiro viewpoint for expansive vistas. A significant historical site is Poço da Neve, an igloo-shaped structure from 1813 used to store snow. Additionally, the Aqueduto dos Pegões is a highlight along the way.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Madeira's climate is generally mild year-round, making the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) enjoyable in most seasons. For the most pleasant conditions, consider hiking during spring or autumn when temperatures are comfortable and the vegetation is vibrant. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed in the provided sources. However, as the trail connects to the Funchal Ecological Park, it's advisable to look for parking options near the park's access points or the Casa do Barreiro, which is mentioned as a point along the route.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed on this specific trail. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on the available information, there is no mention of required permits or entrance fees for hiking the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4). Most levada trails in Madeira are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The PR 4 Levada do Barreiro is described as an 'easy' trail in some contexts, making it accessible for families and those seeking a leisurely walk. Its relatively flat sections and scenic beauty can make it a pleasant outing for children, provided they are comfortable with the distance.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route of the Tour of Anunciada (PR 4) also passes through sections of the Percurso da Anunciada, Caminho de Fátima Nascente, Percurso das Caldelas, Rua Casal da Boavista, and Ponte dos Frades.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's recommended to bring appropriate hiking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing as weather can change. A camera would also be great for capturing the panoramic views.
